item.title: 【Apache Spark 3 to Apache Spark 4 Migration: What Breaks, What Improves, What's Mandatory】Apache Spark 3 到 Apache Spark 4 的迁移：哪些中断、哪些改进、哪些强制
item.description:
【

Apache Spark 4.0 represents a major evolutionary leap in the big data processing ecosystem. Released in 2025, this version introduces significant enhancements across SQL capabilities, Python integration, connectivity features, and overall performance. However, with great power comes great responsibility — migrating from Spark 3.x to Spark 4.0 requires careful planning due to several breaking changes that can impact your existing workloads.

This comprehensive guide walks you through everything you need to know about the Spark 3 to Spark 4 migration journey. We'll cover what breaks in your existing code, what improvements you can leverage, and what changes are mandatory for a successful transition. Whether you're a data engineer, platform architect, or data scientist, this article provides practical insights to ensure a smooth migration path.

】

Apache Spark 4.0 代表了大数据处理生态系统的重大演进飞跃。该版本于 2025 年发布，在 SQL 功能、Python 集成、连接功能和整体性能方面引入了重大增强。然而，能力越大，责任越大 - 从 Spark 3.x 迁移到 Spark 4.0 需要仔细规划，因为一些重大更改可能会影响您现有的工作负载。

这份综合指南将引导您完成有关 Spark 3 到 Spark 4 迁移之旅所需了解的所有信息。我们将介绍现有代码中的哪些问题、可以利用哪些改进以及成功过渡必须进行哪些更改。无论您是数据工程师、平台架构师还是数据科学家，本文都提供了实用的见解，以确保顺利的迁移路径。

item.title: 【AI-Driven Automated Trading System】人工智能驱动的自动交易系统
item.description:
【

In the rapidly evolving field of algorithmic trading, I have observed that access to sophisticated strategies is typically limited to professional traders and large institutions. In my experience, most traditional systems demand deep market knowledge, continuous monitoring, and significant technical expertise, creating barriers that prevent everyday individuals from participating with confidence. Through this article, I share my practical experience designing and implementing a fully automated, AI-driven trading system intended to remove these constraints and allow users, regardless of trading experience or geographic location, to benefit from advanced trading strategies.

The core innovation of the system I built is a hybrid signal fusion engine that combines the Relative Strength Index (RSI), time-series neural networks, and large language models such as Google Gemini and OpenAI GPT to generate, validate, and explain high-confidence trading signals. I implemented the platform on a robust Oracle Database backend with native multi-user support, enabling global users to securely connect their broker accounts and operate autonomously. In my implementation, the system performs real-time market analysis across 1, 5, and 15-minute timeframes, manages signal generation and trade execution, and maintains complete historical records without requiring manual intervention.

】

在快速发展的算法交易领域，我发现只有专业交易者和大型机构才能获得复杂的策略。根据我的经验，大多数传统系统需要深入的市场知识、持续的监控和重要的技术专业知识，这造成了阻碍普通人自信参与的障碍。通过这篇文章，我分享了我设计和实现完全自动化、人工智能驱动的交易系统的实践经验，旨在消除这些限制，并让用户，无论交易经验或地理位置如何，都能从先进的交易策略中受益。

我构建的系统的核心创新是混合信号融合引擎，它结合了相对强弱指数 (RSI)、时间序列神经网络以及 Google Gemini 和 OpenAI GPT 等大型语言模型来生成、验证和解释高可信度的交易信号。我在强大的 Oracle 数据库后端上实现了该平台，并具有本机多用户支持，使全球用户能够安全地连接其经纪商账户并自主操作。在我的实施中，系统在 1 分钟、5 分钟和 15 分钟时间范围内执行实时市场分析，管理信号生成和交易执行，并维护完整的历史记录，无需人工干预。

item.title: 【FinOps for Engineers: Turning Cloud Bills Into Runtime Signals】工程师的 FinOps：将云账单转化为运行时信号
item.description:
【

The bill lands in your inbox. $37,000 this month. Was $29,000 last month. Someone in Finance cc's half the engineering org asking what happened. Engineering doesn't know. Nobody knows. The thread dies with "we'll investigate" and everyone goes back to fighting fires.

Month later, same thing.

】

帐单已发送到您的收件箱。本月 37,000 美元。上个月是 29,000 美元。工程组织的一半财务抄送人员询问发生了什么事。工程不知道。没有人知道。该线程因“我们将调查”而终止，每个人都回去救火。

一个月后，同样的事情。

item.title: 【Enhancing SQL Server Performance with Query Store and Intelligent Query Processing】通过查询存储和智能查询处理增强 SQL Server 性能
item.description:
【

SQL Server performance issues are a common pain point for database administrators. One of my most challenging scenarios occurred after deploying a financial analytics database update. Reports that previously ran in less than 3 minutes suddenly ballooned to over 20 minutes. Key stored procedures started underperforming, and CPU usage spiked to critical levels during peak workloads.   Through careful investigation, I identified query regressions caused by outdated execution plans and parameter sniffing. Instead of applying temporary fixes, I turned to Query Store and Intelligent Query Processing (IQP) to develop a sustainable, long-term solution. This article provides step-by-step instructions for using these tools, including practical examples, my exact investigation process, configuration changes, benchmark results before and after optimizations, and how these changes improved overall performance and stabilized the production environment.

Performance Issue Investigation: Observing Query Regressions  

The performance degradation stemmed from new internal processes introduced into the application, which altered data patterns. Parameter sniffing a common issue where SQL Server cached an execution plan optimized for specific parameters but reused it for parameters with drastically different data distributions caused previously fast queries to slow down.   To pinpoint the bottleneck, I queried the sys.dm_exec_requests and sys.dm_exec_query_stats views, which revealed certain stored procedures with much higher CPU and runtime durations than they had before.   For example, running the following query helped me confirm which plans were underperforming:         

】

SQL Server 性能问题是数据库管理员的常见痛点。我最具挑战性的场景之一发生在部署财务分析数据库更新之后。之前不到 3 分钟的报道突然增加到 20 多分钟。关键存储过程开始表现不佳，CPU 使用率在高峰工作负载期间飙升至临界水平。   通过仔细调查，我发现了由过时的执行计划和参数嗅探引起的查询回归。我没有应用临时修复，而是使用查询存储和智能查询处理 (IQP) 来开发可持续的长期解决方案。本文提供了使用这些工具的分步说明，包括实际示例、我的具体调查过程、配置更改、优化前后的基准测试结果，以及这些更改如何提高整体性能并稳定生产环境。

性能问题调查：观察查询回归

性能下降是由于应用程序中引入了新的内部流程，从而改变了数据模式。参数嗅探是一个常见问题，其中 SQL Server 缓存了针对特定参数优化的执行计划，但将其重新用于数据分布截然不同的参数，导致以前快速的查询速度变慢。   为了查明瓶颈，我查询了 sys.dm_exec_requests 和 sys.dm_exec_query_stats 视图，这些视图显示某些存储过程的 CPU 和运行时持续时间比以前高得多。   例如，运行以下查询帮助我确认哪些计划表现不佳：

item.title: 【Quality Crisis in Robotics and How Video Annotation Fixes It】机器人技术的质量危机以及视频注释如何解决它
item.description:
【

Robots were initially employed to carry out tasks that were rule-based and repetitive. Today, robotics and artificial intelligence have changed that paradigm. AI-enabled robots are capable of perceiving, reasoning, learning, and adapting to dynamic environments because they are trained on high-quality datasets. These include sensor fusion datasets (combining camera, LiDAR, and radar data), action or behavior annotations, video, and image training data, which are crucial for robotics AI. 

The use of AI, reinforcement learning, and computer vision is enabling robots to evolve into intelligent machines. This is evident in their ability to identify pedestrians on a busy street, recognize objects on a factory floor, or navigate warehouse aisles.   

】

机器人最初被用来执行基于规则的重复性任务。如今，机器人技术和人工智能改变了这种范式。人工智能机器人能够感知、推理、学习和适应动态环境，因为它们接受了高质量数据集的训练。其中包括传感器融合数据集（结合摄像头、激光雷达和雷达数据）、动作或行为注释、视频和图像训练数据，这些数据对于机器人人工智能至关重要。 

人工智能、强化学习和计算机视觉的使用使机器人能够演变成智能机器。这在他们识别繁忙街道上的行人、识别工厂车间上的物体或在仓库过道中导航的能力中表现得很明显。

item.title: 【Building End-to-End Payroll Integrations in Workday Using PECI and PICOF】使用 PECI 和 PICOF 在工作日构建端到端薪资集成
item.description:
【

Integrating Workday with third-party payroll systems is crucial for organizations that use Workday HCM but rely on external payroll providers globally. Workday provides Cloud Connect for Third-Party Payroll, a set of pre-built integration frameworks to securely send HR data changes to and receive payroll results from external payroll systems. Two key Workday integration methods in this context are PICOF (Payroll Integration Common Output File) and PECI (Payroll Effective Change Interface). PICOF and PECI serve as the outbound interfaces from Workday to payroll vendors and together with inbound result integrations they enable a full end-to-end payroll data flow. 

This article provides a brief primer on these integration approaches and how a Workday consultant can design an end-to-end payroll integration using PICOF/PECI from sending employee data changes to pulling back payroll results.

】

将 Workday 与第三方薪资系统集成对于使用 Workday HCM 但在全球范围内依赖外部薪资提供商的组织至关重要。 Workday 为第三方薪资提供 Cloud Connect，这是一组预构建的集成框架，用于安全地将 HR 数据更改发送到外部薪资系统并从外部薪资系统接收薪资结果。在这种情况下，两种关键的 Workday 集成方法是 PICOF（薪资集成通用输出文件）和 PECI（薪资有效变更接口）。 PICOF 和 PECI 充当从 Workday 到薪资供应商的出站接口，并与入站结果集成一起实现完整的端到端薪资数据流。 

本文简要介绍了这些集成方法，以及 Workday 顾问如何使用 PICOF/PECI 设计端到端薪资集成，从发送员工数据更改到提取薪资结果。

item.title: 【Schema Evolution in Delta Lake: Designing Pipelines That Never Break】Delta Lake 中的架构演变：设计永不中断的管道
item.description:
【

One common cause of data pipeline failures is schema drift, where upstream data changes its structure unexpectedly. A new field might appear in a JSON feed or a column’s type might change, causing downstream Spark jobs to error out. Delta Lake, an open-source lakehouse technology, addresses this problem with schema enforcement and schema evolution. These features let pipelines adapt to changes gracefully, helping engineers build data workflows that never break due to evolving schemas.

Delta Lake tracks every schema change in its transaction log, so the table schema is saved (in JSON) with each commit. In practice, this means every table version has a full schema snapshot, and you can time-travel or run DESCRIBE HISTORY to see how fields were added, dropped, or modified. Internally, the metadata JSON in each log directory stores the column definitions, and Delta’s versioned log guarantees that a new column append does not require a full rewrite of old data. This built‑in versioning gives us a safety net we can always roll back to or inspect an earlier schema, which is invaluable for troubleshooting and governance

】

数据管道失败的一个常见原因是架构漂移，即上游数据意外更改其结构。 JSON 源中可能会出现新字段，或者列的类型可能会发生变化，从而导致下游 Spark 作业出错。 Delta Lake 是一种开源 Lakehouse 技术，通过模式实施和模式演化解决了这个问题。这些功能使管道能够优雅地适应变化，帮助工程师构建永远不会因模式演变而中断的数据工作流程。

Delta Lake 跟踪其事务日志中的每个架构更改，因此每次提交时都会保存表架构（以 JSON 格式）。实际上，这意味着每个表版本都有完整的架构快照，您可以进行时间旅行或运行 DESCRIBE HISTORY 来查看字段是如何添加、删除或修改的。在内部，每个日志目录中的元数据 JSON 存储列定义，Delta 的版本化日志保证新列追加不需要完全重写旧数据。这种内置的版本控制为我们提供了一个安全网，我们可以随时回滚或检查较早的模式，这对于故障排除和治理非常有价值

item.title: 【Swift Concurrency, Part 2: Parent/Child Relationship, Automatic Cancellation, Task Groups】Swift 并发，第 2 部分：父子关系、自动取消、任务组
item.description:
【

In part one of this series, we considered the foundation of Swift concurrency: a multithreading technique that underlies Swift concurrency, the definition of the Task, and the difference between Task, Task.detached, and managing priorities. If you haven’t read this, check it out here. In this part, we are going to explore Structured Concurrency, the relationship between tasks, how to execute multiple simultaneous tasks, working with TaskGroup, and more.

Lightweight Structured Concurrency

In the first part, we considered how the await is a potential suspension point that stops the current execution and brings control to another object until it finishes. But if operations are independent and can be run simultaneously.

】

在本系列的第一部分中，我们考虑了 Swift 并发的基础：Swift 并发的多线程技术、任务的定义以及任务、Task.detached 和管理优先级之间的区别。如果您还没有阅读过本文，请查看此处。在这一部分中，我们将探讨结构化并发、任务之间的关系、如何同时执行多个任务、使用 TaskGroup 等等。

轻量级结构化并发

在第一部分中，我们考虑了await如何成为一个潜在的暂停点，它停止当前的执行并将控制权交给另一个对象，直到它完成。但如果操作是独立的并且可以同时运行。

item.title: 【Clean Code: Functions and Error Handling in Go — From Chaos to Clarity, Part 1】整洁的代码：Go 中的函数和错误处理 — 从混乱到清晰，第 1 部分
item.description:
【

Introduction: Why Go Functions Are Special

I've reviewed over 1000 pull requests in Go over the past six years, and the same mistakes keep appearing. Remember your first Go code? It probably had dozens of if err != nil checks and 200-line functions that did everything at once. After analyzing over 50 Go projects, I've identified the main beginner problem: They write Go like Java or Python, ignoring the language's idioms.

Common function problems I've seen:

】

简介：为什么 Go 函数很特别

在过去的六年里，我在 Go 中审查了 1000 多个拉取请求，但同样的错误不断出现。还记得您的第一个 Go 代码吗？它可能有数十个 if err != nil 检查和 200 行函数，可以同时完成所有操作。在分析了 50 多个 Go 项目后，我发现了初学者的主要问题：他们像 Java 或 Python 一样编写 Go，而忽略了语言的习惯用法。

我见过的常见功能问题：

item.title: 【Applying Oracle 19c Release Update (RU): A Practical Guide from My DBA Experience】应用 Oracle 19c 版本更新 (RU)：我的 DBA 经验的实用指南
item.description:
【

After working with Oracle databases for more than 15 years, one thing I have learned is that patching is not just a maintenance task, it’s a critical security and stability practice. Many production issues I have seen in enterprise environments could have been avoided simply by keeping databases updated with the latest Release Updates (RUs).

In this article, I will walk through how I typically apply an Oracle 19c Release Update, upgrading a standalone database from 19.3 to 19.20, using a structured approach that I have followed in multiple production environments.

】

在使用 Oracle 数据库超过 15 年之后，我学到的一件事是，修补不仅仅是一项维护任务，它还是一项关键的安全性和稳定性实践。我在企业环境中看到的许多生产问题本来可以通过使用最新版本更新 (RU) 更新数据库来避免。

在本文中，我将介绍我通常如何应用 Oracle 19c 版本更新，使用我在多个生产环境中遵循的结构化方法，将独立数据库从 19.3 升级到 19.20。
